hero_summary: Rahat al-Arwah is a branch of the Sikah family, known for widening the melodic field of the family and creating a broader upper region while the Sikah tonic remains present.
short_identity: A Sikah-family branch that broadens the jawab and upper region while retaining the essence of the Sikah tonic.

quick_facts.karar: The tonic according to the chosen root
quick_facts.ghammaz: Its importance varies according to the practical route
quick_facts.common_start: It begins by stabilizing the tonic, then opens the jawab

what_is_it: This branch is understood inside the Sikah family as a maqam that preserves the essence of the Sikah tonic, but develops a broader upper region and a more open route.

lower.name: Jins Sikah
lower.description: The lower Sikah jins remains the basis that ties this branch to the family.

upper.name: Broad upper region
upper.starts_on: After the tonic has been stabilized
upper.description: Its personality appears through the breadth of the upper region and the prominence of the jawab.

jins_architecture.transition_note: The difference from basic Sikah appears when the jawab opens and the upper region widens.

sayr.summary: It confirms the tonic first, then opens a broader upper route before returning to reaffirm family belonging.
resting_tones: Resting points within the lower jins
Upper stations with a broader presence
motion_notes: The difference from Sikah is determined through the widening of the upper region
One should listen to how the jawab is opened and how the return from it is handled

transpose_explainer: When this branch is transposed to another root, the interval logic and the functions of the tones that give it its identity inside the Sikah family must be preserved.
historical_notes: This branch is understood through its relation to the parent maqam and its difference from it in the upper region.
school_differences: Its explanation may differ according to school or reference.
common_confusions: Listing note names alone is not enough to understand this branch; one must pay attention to movement and points of repose.
listening_notes: Begin by comparing it with basic Sikah.
